candidates.longDescription: University of California Merced Office of Research and Economic Development Request for Expressions of Interest in Accelerators, Incubators, Entrepreneurship Centers, and Small Business Development Centers for Regenerative Agriculture Initiatives I. Overview The University of California Merced Office of Research and Economic Development (UCM ORED) is seeking expressions of interest from accelerators, incubators, entrepreneurship centers, and small business development centers to partner on regenerative agriculture initiatives. candidates.name: State Climate Funds
candidates.desc: The State of California designated $18 million to the University of California (UC) Merced, per AB 179, for climate action research and innovation to address critical needs of the state. The Office of Research, Innovation & Economic Development was designated to administer these funds. A total of $9M in Climate Action Research Seed Funds will be made available through two peer-reviewed rounds of competition. The focus for these funds is on applied research and development projects that will build capacity for climate resilience, adaptation, and mitigation across the State of California. In keeping with UC Merced’s commitment to community and to serving the state’s diverse population, awarded projects explicitly consider the disparate impact of climate change/action on vulnerable communities and incorporate the perspectives of partners/investigators from diverse disciplines, backgrounds, genders, race/ethnicities, and lived experiences.

candidates.name: Spring 2023 Climate Action Seed Funds
candidates.desc: The Spring 2023 Climate Action Seed Funds solicitation was issued in January 2023. Twenty-three proposals were received, requesting a total of $12,925,522. These were of high quality and underwent two rounds of comprehensive review. ORED awarded nine, three small (up to $50K) and six large (up to $1M), projects (listed below) for a total of $4,096,197. These projects are synergistic with the State of California’s climate action priorities outlined in the California Climate Adaptation Strategy.

candidates.name: Fall 2023 Climate Action Seed Funds
candidates.desc: The Fall 2023 Climate Action Seed Funds solicitation was issued in June 2023. Twenty-five proposals were received, requesting a total of $11,785,877. These were of high quality and underwent two rounds of comprehensive review. ORED awarded eleven, two small (up to $100K) and nine large (up to $1M), projects (listed below) for a total of $4,889,426 These projects are synergistic with the State of California’s climate action priorities outlined in the California Climate Adaptation Strategy.

candidates.name: Fall 2023 Climate Action Innovation and Entrepreneurship Proof of Concept Funds
candidates.desc: The UC Merced Fall 2023 Climate Action Innovation and Entrepreneurship Proof of Concept Funds solicitation opened in August 2023. Seven proposals were received, requesting a total of $349,169. These were of high quality and underwent two rounds of comprehensive review. ORED awarded five projects (listed below) for a total of $249,474. These projects are synergistic with the State of California’s climate action priorities outlined in the California Climate Adaptation Strategy.

candidates.name: Spring 2024 Climate Action Innovation and Entrepreneurship Proof of Concept Funds
candidates.desc: The Spring 2024 UC Merced Climate Action Innovation and Entrepreneurship Proof of Concept Funds solicitation opened in February 2024. Ten proposals were received, requesting ~$500,000. These were of high quality and underwent two rounds of comprehensive review. ORED awarded five projects (listed below) for a total of $249,765. These projects are synergistic with the State of California’s climate action priorities outlined in the California Climate Adaptation Strategy.

candidates.name: Research Sustainability Fund
candidates.desc: On June 5, 2025, Chancellor Muñoz committed $1.5 million in campus funds to support faculty researchers who have been impacted by a pause or stoppage in federal grant award funding. This fund is intended to be used over the coming 12-24 months to support faculty researchers as the federal funding landscape continues to be volatile. Unfortunately, depending on the magnitude of federal actions, it is possible that the fund will not meet all needs. The Research Sustainability Fund will address a researcher’s temporary, short-term operational needs to facilitate transition or ramp-down costs resulting from changes in federal funding.
